items.title: What you need to know about tomorrow's ACT public school strike

children.content: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff are going on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that will see all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.

items.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability

children.content: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

image.altText: The CSIRO logo on a sign

items.title: Million-dollar cigarette plot tied to Canberra grandmother's killer

children.content: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

image.altText: An old photo of a man and a woman, nicely dressed, sitting next to each other smiling.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask

children.content: Concerns about anti-social behaviour, construction and poor lighting are shaping perceptions of safety in Civic, but locals remain divided about whether Canberra's CBD feels unsafe after dark.

gallery.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

thumbnail.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

items.title: Bingo player awarded $285k after tripping on cracked tile at Canberra club

children.content: The woman, who was awarded $285,000, rated her pain as 10 out of 10 and spent three nights in hospital.

image.altText: The exterior of a club.

items.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration entirely on 'policy decisions'

children.content: An independent economist warns Canberrans will need to pay higher taxes and charges if the ACT government continues the same amount of spending on infrastructure and services.

image.altText: A man with glasses and a suit and tie sits in a light green chair surrounded by books in his personal library.

items.title: 'Numbers don't add up': Union slams ACT plan to boost paramedics, firefighters

children.content: The ACT government has unveiled a $94.3 million emergency services package, but the union says it still falls short by about 50 staff needed to prevent future station closures.

items.title: Hanson proposes tax break for drilling alongside new gas royalty

children.content: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson in a black turtle neck with her palm pointed out.

items.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thrilling Canberra clash

children.content: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.

image.altText: RIcky Stuart looks on

items.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tasmania farewell by claiming Crows thriller

children.content: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

image.altText: Blake Hardwick raises both arms in the air in celebration

items.title: Drug testing of athletes to expand as influencers fuel illicit peptides boom

children.content: The nation's anti-doping agency reveals its plans to expand drug-testing in sport in response to the soaring popularity of unregulated peptides in the broader community.

image.altText: A woman holds up two small clear vials.

items.title: What is a super El Niño?

children.content: Forecasts are predicting that a "super" El Niño could form later this year.
The severity of these weather patterns tends to depend on ocean temperatures, which we know are rising.
So, there are fears this could be the strongest El Niño ever.
But what exactly is that going to look like for Australia and the world?

image.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

articles.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

image.altText: A cloudy day over the water at Williamstown

gallery.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

thumbnail.altText: Image shows a blue background with clouds and white writing over the top that reads: What is a super El Nino?

items.title: Economists rush to change interest rate forecasts as jobless rate jumps

children.content: All four major banks now expect the RBA to keep the cash rate on hold at its next meeting on June 16, as the unemployment rate spikes to 4.5 per cent.

image.altText: People walk across a street.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it

children.content: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

image.altText: A group of people stand together, raise their fists and hold signs calling for climate justice.

items.title: As Colbert wraps up The Late Show, late-night TV could be on shaky ground

children.content: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

articles.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the US's most important satirists

children.content: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

image.altText: Stephen Colbert sits behind a desk on the Late Show set

A woman holds up a protest sign featuring the CBS eye logo and the phrase "Under his eye" outside the Late Show's theatre.

items.title: How Australia's EV uptake compares with the rest of the world

children.content: High petrol prices are driving EV sales around the world, with the leading international authority predicting 30 per cent of all new cars sold this year will be electric.

image.altText: workers in a car factory

items.title: What are the signs of broken heart syndrome?

children.content: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

image.altText: A woman holding her chest

items.title: Kylie Minogue doco reveals second cancer diagnosis, IVF struggles, and more

children.content: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

items.title: Think you know music and film? Let's see how well you score in this quiz

children.content: From Mozart to Max Richter, can you pick the classical music in these iconic films?

items.title: Support worker cut keys to NDIS participant's home without knowledge, inquiry hears

children.content: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.

image.altText: A close up of an NDIS sign next to a building

items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine gets green light to challenge China's dominance

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

gallery.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

thumbnail.altText: a drone shot of a mining camp in Central Australia.

items.title: 'Will be a deadline': Wagga homeless community urged to take new offer

children.content: The NSW minister for homelessness says people will be offered additional support to move out of camps around Wagga Wagga, but that they will be forced to relocate eventually.

image.altText: woman in his vis yellow vest and hard hat on a contruction site

items.title: Defence minister 'disappointed' by lawsuit over Darwin port ownership

children.content: Chinese-owned company Landbridge launched legal action earlier this year following a pledge by the Prime Minister to return the port to Australian hands. Defence Minister Richard Marles says the government will fight the suit and remained committed to finding a domestic buyer.

image.altText: White man standing in front of podium, light pink shirt, navy blue pants, mid-speech, man behind him in white shirt.

items.title: Israel supported Ben-Gvir's callousness, until it didn't

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

articles.title: Israeli minister posts video taunting detained activists of Global Sumud Flotilla

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

image.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

articles.title: Government calls for detained Australian flotilla members to be released from Israel

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a ship holding a sign calling for someone's freedom, with Palestinian flags flying in background.

gallery.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

thumbnail.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Former NSW Young Australian of the Year dies aged 32

children.content: Nikhil Autar, who was diagnosed with leukaemia when he was 17 years old, was named NSW Young Australian of the Year in 2024.

image.altText: A man in a check shirt with a stethescope around his neck.

items.title: Elon Musk could become world's first trillionaire as SpaceX files for largest IPO ever

children.content: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.

image.altText: SpaceX founder Elon Musk speaks at a press conference

items.title: Female AFL creators say vile comments 'just one example of what we deal with'

children.content: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

gallery.altText: A screen shot of The Jumper Punch podcasters during an episode.

thumbnail.altText: A screen shot of The Jumper Punch podcasters during an episode.

items.title: MND has been called 'the beast'. We still don't know what causes it

children.content: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

image.altText: Jai Arrow close up of him looking up.

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns

children.content: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

image.altText: A police car parked at an angle off a road with police tape in foreground

items.title: Rockhampton hotel fallen into disrepair sells for $5.75 million at auction

children.content: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

items.title: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Abetz looks to rein in Tasmanian spending

children.content: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

image.altText: Treasurer Eric Abetz in front of a budget cuts sign and piles of money.

items.title: Why Raúl Castro has been charged now over a plane downing 30 years ago

children.content: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.

image.altText: A close up, front on shot of an older man with gold rimmed glasses.

items.title: Why 70,000 people could decide future for 70 million Brits

children.content: Andy Burnham is angling to become the UK's next prime minister. He has plenty of popularity, but no seat in parliament. That could be about to change.

items.title: This election just tested Trump's sway over the Republican Party

children.content: Donald Trump's revenge tour is in full swing, and his latest victim is a Republican who has voted with his party more than 90 per cent of the time.

items.title: Ed takes chance another one-in-500-year flood won't hit family home

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mid North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.
